rationale: The citing article uses Ho and Chong (2003) as a concrete empirical example in its argument that behavioral learning models can be estimated and perform well in marketing data. It specifically attributes to that paper an EWA-based product-choice application, better performance than benchmark models, and evidence from a large multi-category purchase dataset. This is more than a passing bibliography mention because the cited study is used to support a key methodological implication section, though it is not directly extended or contested.
relation_description: The review paper draws on Ho and Chong (2003) as evidence that behavioral learning (EWA-style) models can be successfully implemented for consumer choice and can outperform standard benchmark models in scanner-data contexts. The cited work is used as a substantive illustrative support case for empirical applicability.
